title: Mitochondrial rejuvenation as a treatment for adverse age-related conditions and neurodegeneration
abstract: Described herein are methods for mitochondrial transplantation wherein old, damaged, or mutated mitochondria are replaced with young, undamaged, or non-mutated mitochondria. The old, damaged, or mutated mitochondria can also be replaced with mitochondria comprising a desirable mutation or transgene or mitochondria comprising the birth mtDNA sequence. Substituting high functioning mitochondria into cells for low functioning mitochondria can improve function and help resolve conditions caused or propagated by mitochondrial defects that can be caused by aging.
claims: Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A pharmaceutical composition comprising a homoplasmic preparation of human mitochondria in a pharmaceutically compatible excipient. 2. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 1, wherein the homoplasmic preparation of mitochondria is prepared specifically for administration to a particular human subject in need thereof. 3. A method of preparing a pharmaceutical composition according to claim 2, comprising: (a) sequencing the subject's mitochondrial genome with at least 20-fold coverage; (b) determining a consensus sequence of the mitochondrial genome of the subject; and (c) providing a homoplasmic preparation of human mitochondria in which the sequence of the mitochondrial genome is identical to a predetermined subject-specific genome template; wherein the genome template consists of the consensus sequence determined in step (b), or a version of the consensus sequence in which one or more nucleotides in one or more mitochondrial genes has been added, changed, or deleted in comparison with the consensus sequence. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein step (a) comprises sequencing the subject's mitochondrial genomic DNA with at least 100-fold, 500-fold, 2,000-fold, or 10,000-fold coverage. 5. The method of claim 3 or claim 4, wherein step (a) comprises sequencing the genomes of mitochondria obtained from several different tissues in the subject. 6. The method of any of claims 3 to 5, wherein the consensus sequence is a birth mitochondrial genome sequence. 7. The method of any of claims 3 to 6, wherein the genome template of the homoplasmic mitochondria lacks a mutation in a mitochondrial gene identified in the consensus sequence. 8. The method of any of claims 3 to 7, wherein the genome template of the homoplasmic mitochondria contains at least one change in the sequence of a mitochondrial gene that confers improved functional capability to the mitochondria compared with mitochondria endogenous to the subject. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the change confers to the mitochondria at least one property selected from increased stability, resistance to mutation, resistance to elimination, higher membrane potential, and higher energy production capability. 10. The method of any of claims 3 to 9, wherein step (c) includes assembling the genome of the homoplasmic mitochondria from a library of mitochondrial gene fragments. 11. The method of any of claims 3 to 10, wherein step (c) includes preparing multiple copies of the genome template, and inserting the copies into mitochondria from which endogenous DNA has been removed. 12. The method of any of claims 3 to 11, wherein step (c) includes harvesting the homoplasmic mitochondria from a culture of homoplasmic cells. 13. A method of treating an age-related condition or neurodegeneration in a subject, comprising obtaining a preparation of homoplasmic mitochondria according to any of claims 3 to 12, and administering the preparation to the subject. 14 A method of treating an age-related condition or neurodegeneration in a subject, comprising administering to the subject a preparation of mitochondria having improved functional capability compared with mitochondria endogenous to the subject. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the preparation of mitochondria has been prepared by: obtaining cells from the subject; proliferating the cells in vitro; harvesting mitochondria from the proliferated cells; and enriching the harvested mitochondria for mitochondria having higher membrane potential. 16. The method of any preceding clam, wherein the age-related condition is selected from cardiomyopathy, myopathy, dementia, optic atrophy, infertility, organ fibrosis, Parkinson's disease and Alzheimer's disease.
